title: Paraixeris dentata
intro: Paraixeris dentata, also known as Korean toothwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Asteraceae, characterized by its toothed leaves and white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its potential use in the treatment of various health conditions such as digestive issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Paraixeris dentata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Paraixeris dentata is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is classified under the kingdom Plantae, and belongs to the family Asteraceae.
